mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the t-statistic for the return differential versus baseline falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, driving positive equity returns over a quarter as corporate revenues accelerate.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential with a p-value greater than 0.10 over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising policy rates, an accelerating industrial production index signals resilient corporate output and pricing power, supporting positive short-term equity returns despite higher financing costs.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during tightening regimes, and the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High inflation prints are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, and the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates robust labor market absorption and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS and UNRATE divergence signal produces a negative rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, and returns do not outperform a market neutral baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, mechanically translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, and the predictive R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.3 percent signals immediate supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns, prompting negative short-term equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO prints are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of instances, and the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in backtests.
